name: Unsafe Bike Approach
label: Blocked
observedEvidence: Bike approach intersects the busiest vehicle arrival edge.
No protected waiting pocket is shown in the local map layer.
Student observation notes mention turning conflicts.
recommendedFixes: Mark a protected bike arrival box with cones during drop-off.
Move staff supervision to the conflict point for one week of verification.
Request a city review for curb paint, daylighting, and bike-lane protection.

name: Missing Sidewalk Gap
label: Friction
observedEvidence: Local OSM-style layer shows a disconnected pedestrian segment.
The gap sits between a bus stop and the school entrance.
Synthetic census context shows households without vehicles are relevant.
recommendedFixes: Document the gap with daytime photos from a safe location.
Ask facilities and the city to evaluate a temporary walking path.
Prioritize a permanent sidewalk connection in the capital request list.

name: Transit Access Gap
label: Friction
observedEvidence: Frequent transit appears beyond a comfortable five-minute walk shed.
Missing shade and wayfinding increase friction for students carrying bags.
Synthetic demographic context flags non-car access as important.
recommendedFixes: Create a student-tested walking map to the nearest frequent stop.
Ask transit staff about a school-day stop timing adjustment.
Add shade, seating, or wayfinding requests to the corridor improvement list.

name: Compost Placement Gap
label: Blocked
observedEvidence: Bins are placed outside the main disposal line in local observations.
Compost action has high climate relevance for cafeteria waste.
Fix feasibility is high because placement can be tested quickly.
recommendedFixes: Move compost bins directly next to trash during one lunch period.
Use color-coded signs above the disposal line.
Run a two-day contamination check before requesting permanent placement.

name: Pickup-Line Idling Bottleneck
label: Blocked
observedEvidence: Vehicle queue overlaps the dismissal walking path.
Traffic proximity context suggests idling reductions may matter locally.
Observation notes mention multiple signal-cycle delays.
recommendedFixes: Pilot a staggered pickup zone and no-idling reminder signs.
Measure queue length every five minutes for one dismissal week.
Ask administrators to coordinate arrival and dismissal routing with the city.

name: Refill Station Visibility Gap
label: Actionable
observedEvidence: Refill infrastructure exists but is not visible from common student paths.
Signage and announcements can be tested without construction.
High confidence comes from direct walkthrough notes.
recommendedFixes: Add directional signs at gym and courtyard entries.
Mark refill stations on student maps and club announcements.
Track bottle refill counts before and after signage.
